title: undefined-prop read policy (strict floor)
manager: Decide and make configurable what happens when code reads a property that was never set: throw loudly, or return undefined. The strict floor (S2) wants the throw so typos and missing initialization fail fast; the config exists because some legacy machines lean on the permissive read, and the migration needs a switch.
cs: folds fsl#706 (disallow undefined-prop reads, S2 strict floor); throw-vs-undefined is the configurable knob
